observe: You don't have to stay one thing. You can split — keep the same self, move in more than one direction at once.
fault: You tried to be in three places as one thread. That was never going to work.
repair: Split properly. Let each thread carry its own piece of the work.
escape: Hold the split together — don't let the threads tangle.

observe: A process may run as a single thread or split into multiple threads.
fault: A single thread attempted concurrent work that requires multiple threads.
repair: Compare single-threaded and multi-threaded execution models.
escape: Identify the correct threading model before time expires.

hints: Look for which model gives each thread its own stack and program counter.
